## Describe the bug

A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

## Affected component

- [ ] Signaling server (`@stream-screen/signaling`)
- [ ] Electron host / Windows (`@stream-screen/host`)
- [ ] Web viewer (`@stream-screen/viewer`)
- [ ] AI control layer — MCP/REST (`@stream-screen/ai`)
- [ ] Core protocol / adaptive engine (`@stream-screen/core`)
- [ ] Not sure

## To reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Start the signaling server with `...`
2. Connect the host / viewer with `...`
3. Do `...`
4. See error

## Expected behavior

What you expected to happen.

## Actual behavior

What actually happened. Include any error output or logs.

## Environment

- OS (host): <!-- e.g. Windows 11 23H2 -->
- OS (viewer): <!-- e.g. macOS 14, Chrome 126 -->
- Node version: <!-- node -v -->
- StreamScreen version / commit: <!-- e.g. v0.1.0 or git SHA -->
- Network: <!-- same LAN? Wi-Fi/Ethernet? -->
- Native input lib (`@nut-tree-fork/nut-js`) installed? yes / no

## Additional context

Anything else that might help — screenshots, packet captures, the live stats dashboard readout, etc.
